event: Hohenzollern candidature for the Spanish throne becomes public
Ems telegram published; war fever in Paris and Berlin
France declares war on Prussia
Wissembourg: first German victory on French soil
Woerth and Spicheren: French driven from Alsace
Gravelotte-St Privat; Bazaine shut up in Metz
Sedan: MacMahon's army surrenders; Napoleon III captured
Republic proclaimed in Paris; Government of National Defence
Siege of Paris begins
French 6% Morgan loan for 10m pounds floated in London
Metz capitulates: Bazaine surrenders 180000 men
Bombardment of Paris forts begins
German Empire proclaimed at Versailles
Paris capitulates; general armistice
Versailles preliminaries: 5bn franc indemnity and Alsace-Lorraine cession
Paris Commune seizes power
Treaty of Frankfurt signed
Bloody Week ends: Commune crushed
First Thiers rente (2bn francs) heavily oversubscribed
Second Thiers loan (3.5bn francs) oversubscribed roughly twelvefold
Last German occupation troops leave France after early payment of the indemnity
